commit.message:
fix: use bookWaitingList endpoint when session is full

When numberOfBookings >= maxParticipants the request is routed to
/publicBooking/online/bookWaitingList instead of /publicBooking/online/book.
waitingList in the payload is also set to true in this case (previously
it was only true when others were already on the waiting list, which
meant the first person to join the waiting list would hit the wrong
endpoint).

commit.message:
feat: add ?dst and ?src URL params for address-based deep-linking (#405)

Allow third-party sites to link to the OSRM frontend with a pre-set
destination (and optionally an origin) by address string, without
needing to look up coordinates first.

New URL parameters:
  ?dst=<address>  destination address (geocoded on load)
  ?src=<address>  origin address (geocoded on load, optional)

These are one-time deep-link parameters. On page load they are resolved
via Nominatim and replaced in the URL by loc= coordinate pairs through
the existing state machinery. They are ignored when loc= waypoints are
already present, so existing sessions are not affected.

Example usage:
  https://map.project-osrm.org/?dst=Berlin
  https://map.project-osrm.org/?src=Paris&dst=Berlin

Changes:
- src/links.js: parse q.src/q.dst → originAddress/destinationAddress
- src/index.js: geocode address params after LRM init, set waypoints
- test/links.test.js: 10 new unit tests for parse/format behaviour

Closes #337

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Optimize token cache remove path and add filter-first-clone flight for filtered retrieval, Fixes AB#3570409 (#3074)

Fixes
[AB#3570409](https://identitydivision.visualstudio.com/fac9d424-53d2-45c0-91b5-ef6ba7a6bf26/_workitems/edit/3570409)
`removeAccount()`/`removeCredential()` were triggering decrypt-all via
`keySet()` → `getAll()` just to check key existence. Adds a flight-gated
optimization for filtered retrieval and elapsed-time telemetry for
`deleteAccessTokensWithIntersectingScopes`.

## Changes

- **`removeAccount()` / `removeCredential()`**: Replace
`mSharedPreferencesFileManager.keySet().contains(cacheKey)` with O(1)
in-memory `containsKey()` on `mCachedAccountRecordsWithKeys` /
`mCachedCredentialsWithKeys`

- **Flight `ENABLE_FILTER_THEN_CLONE_IN_MEMORY_CACHE`**: When enabled,
`getAccountsFilteredBy()` / `getCredentialsFilteredBy()` filter directly
on uncloned in-memory references, then clone only matching items —
avoiding cloning records that will be filtered out. `getAccounts()` /
`getCredentials()` are unaffected and always return cloned lists.

- **Telemetry**: Add
`elapsed_time_cache_delete_access_tokens_with_intersecting_scopes` span
attribute in `MsalOAuth2TokenCache`; remove unused
`elapsed_time_save_account_shared_preferences`; add
`is_filter_then_clone_enabled` attribute in `getCredentials()`

- **Tests**: `KeySetTrackingStorage` wrapper asserts
`keySet()`/`getAll()` are never called during remove; flight on/off
tests verify clone-only-matching behavior for filtered methods

- **Changelog**: vNext `[PATCH]` entry `#3074`

---------

Root cause: Lead and DM agents had no way to write journal entries
- No write_journal MCP tool existed (update_self only writes brain files)
- agent.Teardown() was only called for worker agents, never for Lead/DM

Fixes:

  Agents call this explicitly to record: task, outcome (success/partial/failure),
  2-4 sentence summary, lessons learned, and skills proposed
- internal/tools/register.go: register WriteJournalTool in the tool set

  filename format from HHMM to HHMMSS (second precision) to prevent collision
  when auto-entry and agent-written entry occur in the same minute

  and decomposePrompt (step 3 of task section) instructing agents to call
  write_journal after every meaningful task or conversation
- internal/orchestrator/orchestrator.go: add automatic fallback goroutine journal
  writes after successful Lead channel turns (len(tasks)==0) and after every
  successful DM turn — guarantees the diary is never empty even if the model
  does not call the tool

Result: every completed interaction now produces at least one journal entry;
agent-written entries are richer, auto-fallback entries ensure nothing is missed

commit.message:
perf(maglev): extend int32 truncation to Generic arithmetic ops

Recognize Generic bitwise/shift ops (GenericBitwiseOr, etc.) as
truncation points in the int32 truncation pass, not just Int32
variants.  Walk backward from these points to convert GenericAdd,
GenericSubtract, GenericMultiply, GenericIncrement, and
GenericDecrement to their wrapping Int32 counterparts when:
1. The node has exactly one non-transparent consumer (truncation chain)
2. All inputs are provably i32-valued (constants, Int32 ops, bitwise
   ops, Phi nodes in smi loops, or already-converted nodes)

This fixes the arithmetic_loop benchmark pattern:
  n = (n + i * 3 - 1) | 0
where the \| 0\ truncation makes all intermediate overflows
irrelevant.  Previously, the smi_guarded 32-bit ADD would deopt on
overflow (sum > 2^31), permanently blocking JIT after 10 retries.
Now the entire chain uses wrapping Int32 ops with no overflow check.

Also reverts the 64-bit smi_guarded fix (0bbe81fd) which caused a
325x regression in property_access/deep_object benchmarks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: rewrite SELECT * as FROM-first DuckDB syntax (#21)

Implements the FROM-first formatting rule.

## What

`SELECT * FROM t` → `FROM t` (and `WHERE`, `ORDER BY`, etc. are
preserved)

## How

- Added `prefer_from_first: bool = True` to `JarifyConfig`
- Overrides `select_sql` in `JarifyGenerator`: when the only expression
is `*` and there are no JOINs, strips the `SELECT` line
- JOINs excluded: sqlglot re-parses `FROM func() JOIN` by attaching the
join to the table node rather than the Select, causing an idempotency
shift — keeping `SELECT *` for joined queries avoids this

## Tests

- `tests/fixtures/select/from_first` — simple, WHERE, ORDER BY cases
- `TestFromFirst` — star converts, JOIN keeps SELECT, DISTINCT keeps
SELECT, explicit columns unaffected, opt-out via config

Resolves #20

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test failures and TypeScript errors

- Refine EnvSubst secret blocklist to allow simple vars like TOKEN
  Focus on env vars with clear secret patterns (API_KEY, ACCESS_TOKEN, etc)
  Allows TEST env vars to work while blocking credential leakage
  Fixes TestEnvSubstHeaderValues test

- Remove MaxBytesReader from response body handling
  MaxBytesReader with ResponseWriter causes 413 errors mid-response
  Request body limit still enforced; response body can be checked at read-time
  Fixes all E2E test failures (HTTPProxyAndHistory, BreakpointPauseAndResume, ReplayRequest)

- Add null checks for tree view providers in extension.ts
  Providers can be undefined after tree view disposal
  Fixes TypeScript compilation errors TS2345 and TS18048

All Go and TypeScript tests now pass.

commit.message:
Rewrite shadow-ai DLP to 4-location model (Devices, Browser, Network, EnterpriseAI)

Consolidate from 8 policies / 37 rules to 5 policies / 13 rules aligned
with the modern Purview DLP locations for generative AI scenarios:

- Shadow AI - Endpoint Protection (Devices): clipboard, upload, drag-drop
- Shadow AI - Browser Prompt Protection (Browser): typed/pasted prompts
- Shadow AI - Network AI Traffic (Network): outbound HTTPS to AI SaaS
- Shadow AI - Enterprise AI Prompt Protection (EnterpriseAI): Copilot SITs
- Shadow AI - Enterprise AI Label Protection (EnterpriseAI): label blocking

Each SIT policy uses 3 Adaptive Protection rules (Elevated=block,
Moderate=warn, Minor=audit) with SSN, Credit Card, Bank Account,
and Medical Terms.

Module: add Browser, Network, EnterpriseAI location mappings to DLP.psm1
Schema: add new locations and policyMode values to all 3 schema files
